title: How long does a kitchen installation take?
metaDescription: Pindi Sahota explains how long a typical kitchen installation takes in Coventry and Warwickshire — from 1 day to 2 weeks depending on scope.
answer: A straightforward swap-out kitchen takes 1–3 days. A full new-build kitchen with plumbing, electrics, and plastering typically takes 7–10 working days. Complexity, kitchen size, and the number of trades involved are the key variables.
bodyHtml:
What affects the timeline?

The biggest factors are whether you need structural changes, how many trades are involved, and whether the kitchen is a like-for-like replacement or a full redesign.

Typical timescales

Like-for-like swap: 1–3 days. Standard new kitchen: 5–7 days. Full renovation with structural work: 10–15 days.

faqs.q: Can a kitchen be installed in one day?
faqs.a: A simple swap-out of doors and worktops can be done in a day, but a full installation typically takes several days.

faqs.q: What causes delays in kitchen installations?
faqs.a: Delayed deliveries, unforeseen structural issues, and unavailable tradespeople are the most common causes.

title: How much does kitchen installation cost?
metaDescription: Honest breakdown of kitchen installation costs in Coventry and Warwickshire from an experienced fitter with 30 years of experience.
answer: Labour alone typically costs £1,500–£4,000+ depending on the complexity of the job. A full kitchen renovation including units, worktops, appliances, and labour can range from £5,000 to £30,000+.
bodyHtml:
What's included in the cost?

Costs cover labour, disposal of old kitchen, and any materials supplied by the fitter. Units, appliances, and worktops are usually supplied separately by the homeowner.

Day rate vs fixed price

Some fitters charge a day rate (£200–£350/day). Pindi offers fixed-price quotes after a £195 pre-installation survey.

faqs.q: Is a fixed-price quote better than a day rate?
faqs.a: A fixed price gives certainty. A day rate can be lower if the job is simple, but costs can escalate if problems arise.
The survey checks your kitchen's dimensions, identifies potential issues, and results in a fixed quote. The fee is credited back when you proceed.

title: Do I need a building regulation certificate for a kitchen?
metaDescription: Find out when you need building regulation certificates for a kitchen installation in England and Wales.
answer: You need a Part P certificate for any new electrical circuits and a Gas Safe certificate for any gas work. These are legal requirements, not optional extras. Your installer should provide them as standard.
bodyHtml:
Part P electrical certification

Any new electrical circuit in a kitchen requires a Part P certificate issued by a registered electrician. Moving sockets or adding a circuit counts.

Gas Safe certification

Gas hob connections, gas ovens, and boiler work must be carried out by a Gas Safe registered engineer and a certificate issued.

faqs.q: What happens if I don't get a Part P certificate?
faqs.a: It could invalidate your home insurance and cause problems when selling your property.
Yes. Pindi coordinates all trades and ensures certificates are provided for electrical and gas work.
